title: 1981-04-16_Pat_98.txt
key_claims: Dick has an "extraordinary revelation": his 2-3-74 experience cannot be viewed in isolation. It is the climax of an authentic 12-year religious cycle that began with a "ritual death" (mescaline and personal tragedy) in 1970.

He maps this cycle onto his novels: *Flow My Tears* (crucifixion), *A Scanner Darkly* (death in the tomb), *VALIS* (sudden resurrection), *The Divine Invasion* (rebirth), and *BTA* (Christogenesis as Ditheon).

The Gospels are not historical biographies but a "paradigmatic recipe" for how any human can become Christ. The Passion and Crucifixion are necessary prerequisites for the ultimate goal: the Nativity (the birth of Christos within the individual).
He finds the ultimate "benchmarks of God" in absolute beauty, describing beauty as an "infinity of infinities" that proves the universe is the product of divine design.
recurring_concepts: **Christogenesis**: The ultimate goal of the "alchemical" process of suffering and rebirth, resulting in the individual becoming a micro-Christ.
**The Template**: Identified as Plato's perfect Ideas (the Logos), to which our entropic, material world falls short, but by which it is measured and regulated.
reading_excerpt: Dick has an "extraordinary revelation": his 2-3-74 experience cannot be viewed in isolation. It is the climax of an authentic 12-year religious cycle that began with a "ritual death" (mescaline and personal tragedy) in 1970.
